headline: How Some Frogs Survive a Deadly Global Fungus
meta_description: Scientists discovered how some frogs survive a deadly fungus: powerful immune defenses developed during their tadpole stage.
summary_short: Researchers have solved the mystery of frog survival against a devastating fungus, uncovering immune defenses that form in tadpoles.
body:
A fungal disease has devastated amphibian populations worldwide, wiping out entire species and leaving scientists searching for answers. Yet some frogs mysteriously survive the infection. A new study has finally revealed their secret.

Surviving frogs develop powerful immune defences, but not after infection strikes—while they are still tadpoles. This early head start, forged in the water before metamorphosis, equips them to resist the fungus once they become adult frogs on land. It's a elegant biological strategy: preparation long before the threat arrives.

The research yielded another unexpected discovery: a vast collection of previously unknown antimicrobial peptides—molecules that kill harmful organisms. These natural compounds, evolved by frogs over millions of years, could eventually inspire new human medicines to fight infections. The findings offer hope: understanding how nature solves the problem of fungal survival might help scientists protect vulnerable amphibian populations, while the biochemistry behind it opens doors to treatments that could benefit many species, including our own.
